metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that per-agentic-pass tool-call counts are available (or specify what new step-recorder instrumentation will expose them) before relying on 'zero tool calls' as the low-grounding signal; the current production metric is only `verify_requests` (model request turns for STEP_VERIFY).
findings.finding: The plan asserts a 'low-grounding observational note when an agentic pass makes zero tool calls' but no production infrastructure tracks per-agentic-pass tool-call counts. In `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`, the only per-step agent metric is `verify_requests` (model requests, not tool calls, and only for the single STEP_VERIFY step). The `_tool_calls` field exists only in experimental harnesses under `docs/experiments/` — it is not wired into the production workflow. The plan treats this as a detectable signal without specifying how it will be measured.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10): 'low-grounding observational note emitted when an agentic pass makes zero tool calls on a NON-TRIVIAL diff'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) scope the step recorder to emit a `tool_calls` count per agent step (alongside `verify_requests`) and confirm the workflow YAML surfaces it, or (b) redefine the low-grounding proxy in terms of `verify_requests == 0` (which IS already tracked) and name the substitute explicitly in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what 'non-trivial diff' means for the plan-review gate specifically: either add a diff-size input to the plan-review gate invocation and specify the named constant against that, or redefine the threshold in terms of a plan-text proxy (e.g., number of ACs, description length) that the gate already has access to.
findings.finding: The plan invokes 'NON-TRIVIAL diff' with the threshold '> 1 changed file OR > 20 changed lines' for the plan-review gate's low-grounding note, but the plan-review gate operates on ticket text (not a diff) and has no `changed_files` or `changed_lines` input. No diff-size signal exists on the plan-review path in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` or `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — `changed_files` is a code-review-gate concept only.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10): 'a diff exceeding a small fixed threshold (proposed: > 1 changed file OR > 20 changed lines)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what metric to read for 'zero tool calls' in the low-grounding note (e.g. add a `pass1_tool_calls` counter to `coverage.metrics` from the agentic batch runner, distinct from `llm_calls`), or redefine the signal in terms of existing emitted data (e.g. `verify_requests == 0` on a non-trivial diff).
findings.finding: The low-grounding observational note (#10 AC) depends on detecting 'zero tool calls on a non-trivial diff' during an agentic pass, but `coverage.metrics` currently records `llm_calls` (criteria-batch count) and `verify_requests` (Pass-2 step count) — neither is a per-step tool-call count for the Pass-1 agentic runners. The instrumentation needed to observe 'zero tool calls' does not exist in the emitted metrics, making this signal unimplementable as described without new instrumentation.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10); AC: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics; low-grounding note emitted using the named non-trivial-diff threshold'
findings.suggested_fix: Redefine the signal in terms of an existing or easily-addable metric: add a `pass1_grounding_calls` field to `coverage.metrics` (incremented by the agentic runner each time it executes a grounding tool), then emit the note when `pass1_grounding_calls == 0` and the diff exceeds the named threshold. Alternatively, reframe as 'no code-grounding criteria ran' (already trackable via the criteria routing skip list).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how per-agentic-pass tool-call counts will be measured in production (e.g. extend the workflow step recorder to capture tool-call counts from the runner's usage data, or define an accepted proxy), and add a fallback (e.g. suppress the note) when the count is unavailable.
findings.finding: The low-grounding observational note (#10) requires detecting 'zero tool calls on a NON-TRIVIAL diff,' but the production workflow recorder (gate_dispatch.py) does not track per-agentic-pass tool-call counts. Only `verify_requests` (model turns for STEP_VERIFY) and `agent_calls` (step count) are recorded; per-pass tool-call counts exist only in experimental harnesses (docs/experiments/), not in production src/. The plan names no fallback if the signal is unmeasurable and scopes no instrumentation work.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10): 'low-grounding observational note emitted when an agentic pass makes zero tool calls on a NON-TRIVIAL diff'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concrete numeric thresholds for the approach-viability trigger (e.g., '≥ N surviving high-priority findings after Pass 3' and/or 'Pass-2 drop rate ≥ X%') so the signal is actually deterministic.
findings.finding: The approach-viability note trigger condition uses undefined thresholds: 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count or high Pass-2 drop rate' — neither 'unusually high' nor 'high' is quantified, so an executor cannot implement the deterministic signal.
findings.location: What / Approach-viability note (#12); AC: 'Approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: e.g., 'Trigger thresholds: surviving_high_priority_count ≥ 3 OR pass2_drop_rate ≥ 40%. Both are named constants in config.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the approach-viability signal trigger thresholds as named constants (e.g., MIN_SURVIVING_HIGH_PRIORITY, MAX_PASS2_DROP_RATE) with initial proposed values stated in the plan, mirroring the non-trivial-diff threshold treatment.
findings.finding: The thresholds that trigger the approach-viability advisory note ('unusually high surviving-high-priority count', 'high Pass-2 drop rate') are described qualitatively but are neither concretely defined nor made configurable, even though they are expected to need tuning as the system matures.
findings.location: What section, item #12; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'Trigger constants (named, configurable): surviving_high_priority_threshold = 3, pass2_drop_rate_threshold = 0.5 — initial values to be adjusted after first 20 runs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete threshold values (or specify them as named constants / config keys) for 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' and 'high Pass-2 drop rate' in the viability note spec — e.g. `VIABILITY_HIGH_PRIORITY_THRESHOLD = 5` and `VIABILITY_DROP_RATE_THRESHOLD = 0.6` — to make the 'deterministic' claim implementable.
findings.finding: The approach-viability note (#12) is described as a 'deterministic post-Pass-3 signal' but the plan names no threshold values for what constitutes 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count' or 'high Pass-2 drop rate'. Without concrete threshold values (or a specification of where they are defined — config, named constants, registry), the signal cannot be implemented deterministically, contradicting the plan's own emphasis on determinism.
findings.location: What / Approach-viability note (#12); AC: 'Approach-viability advisory note emitted from a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define concrete numeric thresholds for the approach-viability note triggers (e.g. surviving-high-priority count ≥ N, Pass-2 drop rate ≥ X%) as named constants co-located with the verdict assembly, so the signal is deterministic and testable.
findings.finding: The approach-viability advisory note (#12) is triggered by 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count OR high Pass-2 drop rate,' but neither threshold is defined anywhere in the plan. These are the decision-rule parameters a deterministic post-Pass-3 signal needs; without numeric definitions the signal is not deterministic.
findings.location: What / Approach-viability note (#12): 'unusually high surviving-high-priority count or high Pass-2 drop rate'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the permitted `root-cause category` enum for FP-ledger tickets (e.g. `false-evidence`, `rubric-overapplication`, `hallucinated-gap`, `scope-mismatch`) so the compile step can group recurring causes into eval no-fire cases deterministically.
findings.finding: The 'root-cause category' referenced in the FP-ledger convention (#7) is an enum whose value vocabulary is never defined anywhere in the plan or codebase. The compile step needs to group recurring root causes into eval no-fire cases, but without an enumerated set of categories an executor cannot implement consistent categorization.
findings.location: What / FP-ledger convention (#7): 'file a bug-typed rebar ticket tagged fp:code-review with the finding + fingerprint + root-cause category'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small, named enum (4–6 values, e.g. `false-evidence | rubric-overapplication | hallucinated-gap | scope-mismatch | threshold-calibration | other`) to the tags/convention doc, and have the compile step key on it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the output format of the compile step (e.g. reference the existing fire/no-fire eval fixture schema in src/rebar/llm/evals/) so the drafted eval cases are compatible with the existing eval harness and the AC is verifiable.
findings.finding: The 'compile step' that 'drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' (AC #1) references a processing protocol whose output format/schema is never specified. The plan does not name the eval-case format the compile step must produce, nor does it reference any existing eval fixture schema (e.g. the fire/no-fire format used by src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Reference the existing eval fixture format (as produced by src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py) and state that the compile step emits one `{criterion_id, expect: 'no-fire', input: <plan_text_snippet>, rationale: <root-cause-category>}` record per ledger ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the compile step should be deferred until ≥3 fp:code-review tickets exist (Rule-of-Three), or gate AC1 as 'convention documented + compile step stub'; document the ledger-ticket schema before building the compiler so real FP data can validate the taxonomy.
findings.finding: The AC unconditionally requires 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets,' but the plan body says the ledger is 'manual to start' — meaning the fp:code-review ticket corpus that feeds the compile step is empty at delivery time (grep confirms zero fp:code-review tickets exist). Building compile-step automation against a zero-instance dataset is a YAGNI / Rule-of-Three violation: the abstraction has no real call-site data to justify it yet.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 1: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented; a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets.'
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC1 into two milestones: (a) 'fp:code-review convention + ticket schema documented' (deliver now), (b) 'compile step ships when ≥3 recurring-cause clusters are observed in the ledger' (deliver when data exists). This preserves the LOOP intent without building automation prematurely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a retirement/lifecycle path for processed FP tickets (e.g., apply a 'compiled' tag, close the ticket, or maintain a processed-cursor) and specify that the compile step is idempotent under re-runs.
findings.finding: The FP ledger (rebar tickets tagged fp:code-review) has no defined retirement or cleanup path, and the compile step's idempotency under re-runs is unspecified — already-processed tickets could be re-drafted into eval cases on every compile run.
findings.location: What section: FP-ledger convention (#7) / AC: 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Tag processed tickets with fp:code-review:compiled after drafting the eval case, and query only tickets lacking that tag; this makes each run idempotent and prevents unbounded accumulation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide and record the non-trivial-diff threshold as a single named value in the plan (e.g., `NON_TRIVIAL_DIFF_THRESHOLD = 20 lines` OR `> 1 file`, not both options open).
findings.finding: The non-trivial-diff threshold is explicitly left undecided: the plan proposes two candidates ('> 1 changed file OR > 20 changed lines') and instructs the executor to 'pick one value', deferring a real design choice.
findings.location: What / Grounding-effort proxy + health telemetry (#10)
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'pick one value' instruction and commit to one: e.g., `NON_TRIVIAL_DIFF_LINES = 20` (lines-based is simpler to implement uniformly across diff formats).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC1's documentation clause as a specific artifact: e.g., 'A `CONVENTIONS.md` (or equivalent) exists at a named path, containing at minimum: tag (`fp:code-review`), required fields (finding, fingerprint, root-cause category), and an example entry.'
findings.finding: AC1's first clause, 'ledger convention documented', is effort-framed rather than specifying an observable artifact or outcome, making it non-measurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'fp:code-review ledger convention documented'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add error handling to the compile step: define timeout, retry behavior, and per-ticket error isolation (skip malformed ticket, log, continue) so one bad ticket or a transient rebar outage doesn't abort the full compile.
findings.finding: The periodic compile step that reads rebar tickets tagged fp:code-review has no error handling specified for rebar unavailability, API errors, or malformed ticket data.
findings.location: AC: 'a compile step drafts eval cases from ledger tickets' / What section: FP-ledger convention (#7)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the file/module where the non-trivial-diff threshold constant should be defined (e.g., a config constant in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` or a dedicated constants module).
findings.finding: The `non-trivial-diff threshold` named constant is described as 'make it a named constant' but the plan names no file or module where this constant should be defined. This is new behavior (CREATION) with an unenumerated edit target.
findings.location: What / #10 grounding-effort proxy; AC line 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `_enrich_verdict_coverage_metrics()` in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` as the concrete edit target for the four new `coverage.metrics` sub-fields, and clarify whether `findings/run` is a per-run count (trivially available) or a rolling aggregate (requiring a separate store).
findings.finding: The plan says 'health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics' but never names the actual function that builds that dict — `_enrich_verdict_coverage_metrics()` in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` — as the edit target. The four new sub-fields (`advisory-vs-blocking ratio`, `findings/run`, `verify-drop rate`, `INDETERMINATE rate`) are absent from the current metrics dict and must be added there, but the edit location is unspecified.
findings.location: What / #10 health-band metrics; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Edit `_enrich_verdict_coverage_metrics()` in `gate_dispatch.py` to emit the four new health-band keys. Note: `findings/run` = len(blocking)+len(surfaced) for that run (no cross-run state needed); `verify-drop rate` = dropped/(dropped+surfaced) if nonzero denominator else 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or artifact assertion to each AC (e.g., for AC2: 'run `make emit-metrics` against a sample diff >20 lines; confirm `coverage.metrics` contains the four named fields'; for AC3: 'run Pass-3 harness with a fixture exceeding threshold; assert advisory note present in output').
findings.finding: No AC has a concrete proving command or check that produces evidence of success; all three ACs describe desired states without specifying how to verify they are met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all three bullets)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section covering (a) the post-Pass-3 approach-viability signal's design rationale and (b) the new metric emission contract with coverage.metrics, so these architectural additions are discoverable and justified.
findings.finding: The approach-viability note (new post-Pass-3 signal in verdict assembly) and health-band metric emission (new integration into coverage.metrics) are new architectural additions to the review pipeline, but neither receives an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry — only inline prose description.
findings.location: Scope section; AC lines 2–3
findings.suggested_fix: Create a single lightweight ADR (or append to an existing design doc) with two sub-sections: one for the verdict-assembly viability note (trigger condition, output format, advisory-only guarantee) and one for the coverage.metrics health-band emission (metric names, semantics, who reads them).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief calibration note: specify that the initial threshold value will be validated against a sample of N recent diffs (e.g., last 20 runs) before being locked in, or explicitly document the reasoning for the chosen value so future tuners have a baseline.
findings.finding: The non-trivial-diff threshold is asserted as 'proposed: > 1 changed file OR > 20 changed lines' with no empirical basis or measurement plan, making it an unvalidated heuristic whose correctness (catching genuine low-grounding passes while avoiding noise) cannot be established by reasoning alone.
findings.location: What section, item #10

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify graceful degradation for coverage.metrics emission failures (e.g., log a warning + emit a named error counter) so a sink outage is visible rather than silently swallowed.
findings.finding: The health-band metric emission to coverage.metrics has no error handling or graceful degradation specified. If the metrics sink is unavailable or the write fails, the failure is silently swallowed with no fallback.
findings.location: AC: 'Health-band metrics emitted on coverage.metrics' / Scope: 'metric emission on coverage.metrics'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap metric emission in a try/except (or equivalent); on failure, emit a structured log line at WARN level (e.g., `telemetry_emit_failed{reason=...}`) and, if possible, a fallback counter increment so the failure itself is observable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider whether the three concerns (FP ledger convention/tooling, health telemetry metric emission, and post-Pass-3 viability note) should be tracked as separate child tickets, since each is independently valuable, testable, and releasable with a distinct actor/reason-to-change.
findings.finding: The plan bundles three independently-releasable outcomes that each have a distinct actor/reason-to-change: (1) the FP ledger is a human-reviewer convention + compile tooling concern, (2) health telemetry is an automated metric-emission concern, and (3) the approach-viability note is a post-Pass-3 verdict-assembly concern. Each could ship and deliver value on its own without the others.
findings.location: ## What — items #7, #10, #12; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all five `finalize_verdict()` call sites (`workflow_ops.py` ×2, `gate_dispatch.py` ×3, `drift_refresh`) as edit targets or explicitly confirm the new viability field is passthrough-safe (added to coverage dict rather than the top-level verdict dict) so no caller update is needed.
findings.finding: The approach-viability note is described as 'a small post-decide note in the verdict assembly', which resolves to `finalize_verdict()` in `orchestrator.py`. That function is called from five distinct sites outside the plan_review directory (`workflow_ops.py` ×2, `gate_dispatch.py` ×3) and from `drift_refresh`. If the plan adds a new key to the returned verdict dict, these callers and their downstream consumers (tests, schema validators, sidecar serialization) are unenumerated.
findings.location: What / #12 Approach-viability note; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add the note to `coverage` (e.g. `coverage['viability_note']`) rather than the top-level verdict dict — coverage is already a pass-through dict and no caller destructures it, so no call-site changes are needed. State this in the Scope section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for using a rebar ticket (vs a sidecar event or direct eval-file append) as the FP ledger substrate — e.g. human visibility in the tracker, the ability to add root-cause commentary, or team workflow alignment.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why the FP ledger uses a rebar ticket (tagged `fp:code-review`) as its substrate, rather than the already-established `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar event infrastructure or a direct append to the eval-spec YAML files. The codebase already has a reducer-ignored sidecar pattern specifically designed for observability feedback, making the choice non-obvious without a stated reason.
findings.location: What / FP-ledger convention (#7)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
